entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: DB Cooper
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown.
In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Mary Celeste
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Hinterkaifeck Murders
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: The Black Dahlia
entries.hook: Decades later we still don't know:
stories: In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.

Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.

In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: The Hinterkaifeck Murders
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: The Roanoke Colony
entries.hook: Decades later we still don't know:
stories: In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

The Circleville Letters. Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.

stories: In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 1966 two Brazilian engineers were found dead on a hilltop wearing lead masks with a cryptic notebook. The cause of death was never determined.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Black Dahlia
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: The case went cold: Elisa Lam
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Yuba County Five
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

entries.title: The case went cold: Flight 370
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: The Black Dahlia
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: Elisa Lam
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.

entries.title: The case went cold: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: The Roanoke Colony
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.

entries.script: DB Cooper. In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown. The Lead Masks Case. In 1966 two Brazilian engineers were found dead on a hilltop wearing lead masks with a cryptic notebook. The cause of death was never determined. Flight 370. In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: The Mary Celeste
entries.hook: Decades later we still don't know:
stories: In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: Jack the Ripper
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Roanoke Colony
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: The Sodder Children
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: The case went cold: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: The case went cold: Jack the Ripper
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: The Roanoke Colony
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: Jack the Ripper
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: The Black Dahlia
entries.hook: Decades later we still don't know:
stories: In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Tamam Shud Case
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Tamam Shud Case
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
In 1966 two Brazilian engineers were found dead on a hilltop wearing lead masks with a cryptic notebook. The cause of death was never determined.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.

In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Yuba County Five
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: Jack the Ripper
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: The Mary Celeste
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

stories: In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Tamam Shud Case
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.

entries.title: The case went cold: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.

entries.title: The case went cold: DB Cooper
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown.
In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.

entries.title: The case went cold: Jack the Ripper
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Black Dahlia
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: DB Cooper
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown.
In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.

stories: In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.

stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Lead Masks Case
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1966 two Brazilian engineers were found dead on a hilltop wearing lead masks with a cryptic notebook. The cause of death was never determined.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: Jack the Ripper
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: DB Cooper

entries.title: The case went cold: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: DB Cooper
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown.
In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.

stories: In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown.
In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: The Hinterkaifeck Murders
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.

stories: In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown.
Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: The Sodder Children
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: Decades later we still don't know:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Sodder Children
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Roanoke Colony
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: The case went cold: Jack the Ripper
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

stories: In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: Jack the Ripper
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: DB Cooper
entries.hook: Decades later we still don't know:
stories: In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown.
In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1966 two Brazilian engineers were found dead on a hilltop wearing lead masks with a cryptic notebook. The cause of death was never determined.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Zodiac Killer

stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: The Hinterkaifeck Murders
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.
In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.

Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Hinterkaifeck Murders
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
In 1966 two Brazilian engineers were found dead on a hilltop wearing lead masks with a cryptic notebook. The cause of death was never determined.
In 2014 Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared with 239 people on board. Despite the largest search in aviation history the main wreckage was never found.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: The Black Dahlia
entries.hook: Decades later we still don't know:
stories: In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 1966 two Brazilian engineers were found dead on a hilltop wearing lead masks with a cryptic notebook. The cause of death was never determined.

stories: In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.

entries.title: The case went cold: Jack the Ripper
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.
In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: The case went cold: Jack the Ripper

stories: In 1888 a serial killer murdered at least 5 women in London's Whitechapel district. Despite one of history's largest manhunts Jack the Ripper was never caught.

In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.

entries.title: The case went cold: DB Cooper
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown.
On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.

In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Mary Celeste
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1872 the merchant ship Mary Celeste was found adrift fully intact with food on the table. All 7 crew members and the captain's family had vanished without a trace.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.
In 1959 nine hikers died mysteriously in the Ural Mountains. Their tent was cut from inside and they fled into snow without proper clothing. Some had bizarre injuries including radiation traces.

stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.

In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Black Dahlia
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1947 aspiring actress Elizabeth Short was found murdered in Los Angeles her body cut in half. Despite hundreds of suspects the killer was never identified.
In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
In 1948 a well-dressed man was found dead on an Australian beach with no ID. All clothing tags were removed. Despite DNA testing in 2021 his identity is still debated.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: The Sodder Children

stories: On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.

entries.script: DB Cooper. In 1971 a man calling himself Dan Cooper hijacked a Boeing 727 collected $200,000 in ransom then parachuted out over the Pacific Northwest. Despite 50 years of investigation his identity remains unknown. The Yuba County Five. In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances. Elisa Lam. In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.

entries.title: To this day no one has the answers: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: To this day no one has the answers:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
On Christmas Eve 1945 a fire destroyed the Sodder home. Five children were missing with no remains found. Years later a photo surfaced showing one child alive.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.

entries.title: Decades later we still don't know: The Tamam Shud Case
entries.hook: Decades later we still don't know:
stories: In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.
Starting in 1976 someone terrorized Circleville Ohio with anonymous letters revealing intimate details. One man was convicted but letters continued after he was imprisoned.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: Investigators are still baffled: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: Investigators are still baffled:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1922 six people were murdered on a remote German farm. Days earlier the family reported strange footprints voices in the attic and a newspaper no one bought. No one was ever convicted.
In 1966 two Brazilian engineers were found dead on a hilltop wearing lead masks with a cryptic notebook. The cause of death was never determined.

entries.title: The case went cold: Amelia Earhart
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: In 1937 famed aviator Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific while attempting to fly around the world. Despite the largest naval search in history no trace of her plane was ever found.
In 1587 115 English settlers landed on Roanoke Island. Three years later they were all gone. The only clue was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a tree.
In 1948 an unidentified man was found dead on an Australian beach with a scrap of paper reading 'Tamám Shud.' The paper was torn from a rare poetry book containing an unbreakable code.

entries.title: This mystery has never been solved: The Yuba County Five
entries.hook: This mystery has never been solved:
stories: In 1978 five men drove into Plumas National Forest. Their car was found abandoned. Four were found dead miles apart under bizarre circumstances.
In 1957 a young boy's body was found in a cardboard box in Philadelphia. He was clean and neatly dressed. Despite decades of investigation his identity and killer remain unknown.
Days before the 1922 Hinterkaifeck murders the family reported strange events. The killer lived in the house for days before the murders. No motive or culprit was ever found.

entries.title: The case went cold: The Zodiac Killer
entries.hook: The case went cold:
stories: Between 1968 and 1974 the Zodiac killer murdered at least 5 people in Northern California. He taunted police with cryptic ciphers some never solved. His identity is still unknown.
In 2013 Elisa Lam was found dead in a water tank on the roof of the Cecil Hotel. Surveillance showed her acting erratically in an elevator. How she got to the locked roof remains unexplained.
In 1992 three women vanished from their Springfield Missouri home. Their purses keys and cars were left behind. Only a broken porch light suggested anything was wrong.
